title: Power BI SharePoint CSV Data Source — Folder Connector, Authentication, Scheduled Refresh

confirmed_facts.fact: Use 'SharePoint Folder' connector (SharePoint.Files) to connect to CSVs in a document library. URL must be root site only (e.g. https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite) — do NOT include library path. Use [ApiVersion=15] for SharePoint Online.
SharePoint.Files returns columns: Content (binary), Name, Extension, Date accessed, Date modified, Date created, Attributes, Folder Path. The Content column holds the file binary for parsing.

Authentication: use Organizational Account (OAuth2) for interactive Power BI Desktop. Service principals CANNOT authenticate to SharePoint Online for scheduled refresh — use a licensed M365 user account.
SharePoint Online is a cloud source — no on-premises data gateway required. Schedule up to 8 refreshes/day (Pro) or 48/day (Premium).

When a new CSV is added to the SharePoint folder, it is automatically included in the next scheduled refresh — no Power BI changes needed. The M query dynamically re-reads all files matching the filter.

The built-in 'Combine Files' wizard auto-generates: a Transform Sample File query, a Transform File custom function, and a main combining query. Manual M is preferred when extracting dates from filenames or handling variable schemas.

implementation_notes:
Connect and combine CSVs from SharePoint:
```m
let
    Source = SharePoint.Files("https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite", [ApiVersion=15]),
    InFolder = Table.SelectRows(Source, each Text.Contains([Folder Path], "/Shared Documents/Reports/")),
    CSVOnly = Table.SelectRows(InFolder, each [Extension] = ".csv"),
    AddFile = Table.AddColumn(CSVOnly, "SourceFile", each [Name]),
    Parsed = Table.AddColumn(AddFile, "ParsedCSV",
        each Csv.Document([Content], [Delimiter=",", Encoding=65001, QuoteStyle=QuoteStyle.None])),
    Promoted = Table.TransformColumns(Parsed,
        {"ParsedCSV", each Table.PromoteHeaders(_, [PromoteAllScalars=true])}),
    Expanded = Table.ExpandTableColumn(Promoted, "ParsedCSV",
        Table.ColumnNames(Promoted{0}[ParsedCSV]))
in
    Expanded
```

Combine Files wizard steps (no-code):
1. Get Data > SharePoint Folder
2. Enter site root URL, click OK
3. Authenticate with Organizational Account
4. In Navigator, select folder row > Combine > Combine & Transform Data
5. Configure delimiter/encoding in dialog, click Transform data
6. Auto-generates: 'Transform Sample File' query + 'Transform File' function + main combining query
7. Source.Name column contains original filename — extract month/year from it

Scheduled refresh setup:
1. Power BI Service > dataset > Settings > Data source credentials
2. Sign in with OAuth2 (M365 user with Read access to SharePoint site)
3. Schedule refresh > Configure daily schedule (up to 8/day Pro, 48/day Premium)
4. No gateway needed for SharePoint Online

Auto-trigger refresh on new file upload (Power Automate):
- Trigger: 'When a file is created in a folder' (SharePoint connector)
- Action: 'Refresh a dataset' (Power BI connector)
- This triggers a Power BI refresh whenever a new CSV is uploaded to the monitored folder

Incremental refresh for large file sets:
```m
// Add RangeStart and RangeEnd parameters (type DateTime)
// Filter which files to load based on filename-extracted date:
FilterByRange = Table.SelectRows(CSVOnly, each
    let
        yr = Number.FromText(Text.Middle([Name], 7, 4)),
        mo = Number.FromText(Text.Middle([Name], 12, 2)),
        fileDate = #date(yr, mo, 1)
    in
        fileDate >= Date.From(RangeStart) and fileDate < Date.From(RangeEnd))
```
Note: SharePoint doesn't support query folding — incremental refresh reduces data loaded per refresh but still reads file listing.
caveats: SharePoint.Files URL must be root site only — including library path causes authentication errors
Filenames containing # % $ cause authentication errors due to URL truncation — rename files
Service principals cannot authenticate to SharePoint for scheduled refresh — must use M365 user OAuth2
Single CSV auto-sync (~hourly) cannot be configured for frequency — use folder connector for control
SharePoint.Files returns files from ALL subfolders — always filter by Folder Path
All files being combined must have the same column schema — varying headers cause ExpandTableColumn errors
OAuth token may expire during long refreshes (>1 hour) — use incremental refresh for large datasets
Filter out temp files: not Text.StartsWith([Name], "~") to skip Office temp files
